title: New Homes Get Solar Panels and Heat Pumps
summary: In England, the government has made a new rule for building homes. Starting in 2028, all new houses must have solar panels on their roofs and use heat pumps instead of gas for heating. Solar panels use sunlight to make electricity, and heat pumps are machines that keep homes warm without burning fossil fuels. This change is part of a plan called the Future Homes Standard to make homes better for the environment. It might cost more to build these homes, but it can save money on energy bills over time. Some people like this idea because it helps fight climate change, but builders are worried about the extra cost. The government says it's important to use clean power to stay safe and not rely on other countries for energy.

background_read: This article talks about new rules for building homes in England to make them more environmentally friendly. It explains how solar panels and heat pumps work and why they are important for reducing pollution and saving energy. The rule is part of a bigger plan to improve homes and help fight climate change, but it might increase costs for builders while saving money for homeowners in the long run.
Article_Structure: WHO: The government and builders in England
WHAT: New homes must have solar panels and heat pumps starting in 2028
WHY: To use clean power, save energy, and help the environment

perspectives.description: The government thinks this rule is important for energy security and fighting climate change.
Builders are worried about the extra cost of adding solar panels and heat pumps to new homes.
